title: The Penalty

evidence.note: Published 1920; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Penalty” (renewals ~1947, 1948, 1949)
Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Penalty is a 1920 American silent crime melodrama directed by Wallace Worsley and produced by Goldwyn Pictures, starring Lon Chaney as a criminal mastermind who lost his legs to a childhood surgical error. As a 1920 publication, the film print is in the US public domain by expiry of the 95-year copyright term. Score and restoration layers are left undetermined pending their own evidence.
